what: 1. Top 1 per cent income share (Atkinson-Leigh style)
title: Canonical replications: top 1% share, corporate ETR, tax gap trend
text: approach loosely mirrors Atkinson Leigh (2007), “Distribution Top Incomes Australia,” Economic Record, 83(262), 247-261 (https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1475-4932.2007.00412.x). Atkinson-Leigh method reconstructs top-income shares ATO published tabulations; postcode-based approximation cruder data-proxy. rigorous top-share work use Pareto interpolation ATO top-percentile table apply ALife microdata access. See also Burkhauser, Hahn Wilkins (2015) caveats (https://doi.org/10.1007/s10888-014-9281-z).
code: library(ato) ato_snapshot("2026-04-24")  pc_panel <- ato_individuals_postcode(   year = c("2015-16", "2016-17", "2017-18", "2018-19",            "2019-20", "2020-21", "2021-22", "2022-23") ) pc_panel <- ato_harmonise(pc_panel)  # For each year, rank postcodes by mean taxable income per return, # take top 1% of returns, compute their share of total income. top1 <- function(df) {   df <- df[order(-df$taxable_income / df$number_of_individuals), ]   cum_returns <- cumsum(df$number_of_individuals)   total_returns <- sum(df$number_of_individuals, na.rm = TRUE)   cutoff <- which(cum_returns >= 0.01 * total_returns)[1]   sum(df$taxable_income[seq_len(cutoff)], na.rm = TRUE) /     sum(df$taxable_income, na.rm = TRUE) }  shares <- by(pc_panel, pc_panel$year, top1) shares

what: 2. Corporate effective tax rate by industry (transparency data)
title: Canonical replications: top 1% share, corporate ETR, tax gap trend
code: ctt <- ato_top_taxpayers(year = "2022-23")  # Effective tax rate = tax payable / taxable income, for entities # with positive taxable income. Drop zero-taxable rows (they bias # the ratio; rely on loss-makers analysis separately). ctt <- ctt[!is.na(ctt$taxable_income) & ctt$taxable_income > 0, ] ctt$etr <- ctt$tax_payable / ctt$taxable_income  by_industry <- aggregate(etr ~ entity_type, data = ctt, FUN = median) by_industry[order(-by_industry$etr), ]

title: Download and Tidy Australian Taxation Office Data
text: Australian Taxation Office Commonwealth agency responsible collecting federal taxes. 2023-24 collected AUD 611 billion net tax (ATO Annual Report 2023-24), equivalent around 87% total Commonwealth receipts. administers personal income tax, company tax, GST, fringe benefits tax, fuel tax credits, Research Development Tax Incentive, Super Guarantee compliance regime, self-managed superannuation funds (SMSFs), Corporate Tax Transparency regime. Superannuation policy sits Treasury; prudential regulation APRA-regulated super funds APRA’s domain; conduct disclosure ASIC’s. ATO’s flagship public data release Taxation Statistics: annual XLSX-heavy publication covering roughly 14 million individual tax returns, 1 million company returns, APRA-regulated self-managed superannuation funds. release ships 90-plus tables across Individuals, Companies, Partnerships, Trusts, Super, GST, FBT, CGT, Excise, Activity Statement Ratios, plus separate Corporate Tax Transparency release multi-year postcode series. Beyond Taxation Statistics, 42 ATO datasets data.gov.au include Tax Gaps estimates, Small Business Benchmarks, International Related Party Dealings (IRPD), R&D Tax Incentive claimants, HELP repayments, foreign-ownership register. Taxation Statistics underlies much public income-distribution top-incomes literature Australia. Atkinson Leigh (2007) used ATO aggregates reconstruct century-long top-income shares; Burkhauser-Hahn-Wilkins Wilkins series followed. Work Grattan Institute, e61 Institute, ANU Tax Transfer Policy Institute, Australia Institute routinely draws tables, Corporate Tax Transparency release drives October-November news cycle every year journalists tally large entities paid zero tax. longitudinal microdata needed, researchers apply access ALife (ATO Longitudinal Information Files) ATO’s DataLab; Parliamentary Budget Office accesses richer microdata separate arrangements.

title: Download and Tidy Australian Taxation Office Data
text: ATO’s annual Taxation Statistics publication free available Creative Commons, getting R always tedious. ATO’s statistics site ato.gov.au renders via JavaScript blocks bulk crawlers. canonical machine interface data.gov.au CKAN mirror, CKAN resource UUIDs change every annual release, column names drift year year, table numbers shift (occupation data Table 13, 14, 15 different years), historical series 1994-95 2008-09 ZIP bundle legacy .xls files. Resource URLs resolved runtime via package_show, package doesn’t break CKAN UUIDs rotate. Year accepted sensible form ("2022-23", "2022/23", 2022, "latest"). Column names cleaned snake_case. Data frames carry provenance (source URL, retrieval date, licence) lightweight ato_tbl S3 class.
code: # Without this package api  <- "https://data.gov.au/data/api/3/action/package_show?id=taxation-statistics-2022-23" resp <- jsonlite::fromJSON(api) res  <- resp$result$resources url  <- res$url[grep("postcode", res$name, ignore.case = TRUE)][1] path <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx") download.file(url, path) df   <- readxl::read_excel(path, sheet = 1) names(df) <- tolower(gsub("[^a-z0-9]+", "_", tolower(names(df)))) # ... rename, strip totals rows, parse postcodes as characters, ...  # With this package library(ato) ato_individuals_postcode(year = "2022-23", state = "NSW")

title: ato: Australian Taxation Office Data — ato-package
text: ato_catalog(), ato_download(): discovery generic resource access. ato_individuals(), ato_individuals_postcode(), ato_individuals_occupation(): individual tax return items. ato_individuals_postcode() accepts vector years time-series panels. ato_companies(): company tax across Tables 1-9 (snapshot, size, entity type, industry, industry--size, sub-industry, taxable status, source, expenses). ato_super_funds(): APRA-regulated super funds SMSFs. ato_top_taxpayers(): Corporate Tax Transparency release (Income tax PRRT sheet). ato_gst(), ato_industry(): GST industry aggregates. ato_tax_gaps(): annual Tax Gap estimates across tax heads. ato_rdti(): Research Development Tax Incentive claimants. ato_irpd(): International Related Party Dealings (BEPS / transfer pricing). ato_excise(): excise rates, fuel tax credits, beer spirits clearances. ato_sme_benchmarks(): Small Business Benchmarks. ato_help(): HELP / AASL / VSL student-loan statistics. ato_cite(): citation helper (BibTeX, APA, plain text). ato_cache_info(), ato_clear_cache(): cache management.

what: Caveats for users of ATO data
title: ato: Australian Taxation Office Data — ato-package
text: Nominal AUD. monetary values nominal Australian dollars reporting year. Use inflateR::inflate() ABS CPI series real-term comparisons across years. Fiscal year convention. "2022-23" refers Australian income year 1 July 2022 30 June 2023. Confidentiality suppression. ATO replaces values "np" (published), "*", "\u2021", similar tokens fewer ten taxpayers (fewer 50 returns postcode data) fall cell. package coerces NA numeric columns stay numeric. Summing column na.rm = TRUE correct default; explicit suppressed cells distributional analysis. Schema drift. Column names table numbering shift year--year (e.g. occupation data T13, T14, T15 different years). package cleans names snake_case ingestion normalise schemas across years. cross-year join requires explicit column mapping. Classification migration. ANZSIC 2006 ANZSIC 2020 ANZSCO 2013 ANZSCO 2021 recodes affect industry occupation series. Users stacking across releases inspect classification version used release. Silent revisions. CKAN metadata_modified timestamps change without version bump ATO republishes corrected file. Run ato_clear_cache() force refresh. Microdata scope. 2% Individual Sample File distributed separately Hugh Parsonage's taxstats DRAT repo. ALife (ATO Longitudinal Information Files) restricted-access microdata via ATO DataLab requires researcher application; provided package. Citation. Use ato_cite() returned ato_tbl produce Treasury-grade footnote, APA reference, BibTeX entry source URL, licence, retrieval date, title.

what: Deflate nominal AUD to real AUD — ato_deflate
title: Deflate nominal AUD to real AUD — ato_deflate
text: Converts numeric vector nominal AUD figures indexed financial year real AUD chosen base year using bundled ABS CPI series (annual, Groups Australia, 2011-12 = 1.0). user's inflateR workflow non-Australian contexts, bundle matching CPI series call custom cpi = argument.

title: Deflate nominal AUD to real AUD — ato_deflate
code: ato_deflate(x, year, base = "2022-23", cpi = NULL)

title: Deflate nominal AUD to real AUD — ato_deflate
text: x Numeric vector nominal AUD values. year Character vector financial years entry x, "YYYY-YY" form. Must length x. base Base financial year real terms (default "2022-23"). cpi Optional override: data frame columns financial_year cpi_all_groups_australia. Default uses bundled ABS series.

title: Deflate nominal AUD to real AUD — ato_deflate
text: Numeric vector real AUD values base-year prices.

title: Deflate nominal AUD to real AUD — ato_deflate
text: Uses proportional (Laspeyres-style) adjustment: \(real = nominal \times (CPI_{base} / CPI_{source})\). bundled CPI ABS annual Groups Australia index published cat. 6401.0, rebased 2011-12 = 1.000. standard rebasing used Australian time-series work consistent ABS System National Accounts methodology (cat. 5204.0). formula exact chain-linked index 1949 (ABS CPI introduced) approximate earlier values rely Commonwealth Statistician retail-price series. Use custom cpi = argument need different deflator (e.g. GDP deflator, wage price index, industry-specific PPI).

title: Deflate nominal AUD to real AUD — ato_deflate
text: Australian Bureau Statistics (2024). Consumer Price Index, Australia: Concepts, Sources Methods. Catalogue 6461.0. Australian Bureau Statistics (2024). Consumer Price Index, Australia. Catalogue 6401.0. Diewert, W.E. (1998). "Index Number Issues Consumer Price Index." Journal Economic Perspectives, 12(1), 47-58. doi:10.1257/jep.12.1.47

title: Prepare a Zenodo deposit payload for the session manifest — ato_deposit_zenodo
text: Builds JSON metadata payload Zenodo expects data deposit, using current ato_manifest() snapshot pin set via ato_snapshot(). function upload default; returns payload saved manifest path can inspect calling upload = TRUE.

title: Prepare a Zenodo deposit payload for the session manifest — ato_deposit_zenodo
code: ato_deposit_zenodo(   title = NULL,   description = NULL,   creators = list(list(name = "Anonymous")),   keywords = c("ATO", "taxation", "Australia", "reproducibility"),   upload = FALSE,   sandbox = FALSE,   token = Sys.getenv("ZENODO_TOKEN") )

title: Prepare a Zenodo deposit payload for the session manifest — ato_deposit_zenodo
text: title Deposit title. Defaults "ATO data snapshot YYYY-MM-DD" using current snapshot pin. description Free-text description. Defaults short auto-generated note listing datasets fetched. creators List creator records. list name, optional affiliation, orcid. Defaults single anonymous entry; override published work. keywords Character vector keywords. Defaults c("ATO", "taxation", "Australia", "reproducibility"). upload Logical; TRUE, POSTs deposit Zenodo uploads manifest CSV. Default FALSE (dry run). sandbox Logical; TRUE, uses Zenodo Sandbox (sandbox.zenodo.org) testing. Default FALSE. token Zenodo personal access token. Defaults Sys.getenv("ZENODO_TOKEN").

title: Prepare a Zenodo deposit payload for the session manifest — ato_deposit_zenodo
text: list payload (JSON metadata), manifest_path (CSV manifest staged), upload = TRUE, deposit_id, doi_prereserve, url.

title: Prepare a Zenodo deposit payload for the session manifest — ato_deposit_zenodo
text: upload, supply Zenodo personal access token via ZENODO_TOKEN environment variable (token argument). Tokens can generated https://zenodo.org/account/settings/applications/.

what: Fetch CKAN metadata for an ATO dataset — ato_meta
title: Fetch CKAN metadata for an ATO dataset — ato_meta
text: Returns structured metadata ATO dataset data.gov.au: title, notes, licence, last-modified timestamp, resource count, resource URLs. Useful detecting silent updates clearing cache, auditing version data .

title: Fetch CKAN metadata for an ATO dataset — ato_meta
code: ato_meta(x)

title: Fetch CKAN metadata for an ATO dataset — ato_meta
text: x Either ato_tbl (returned ato_* data function) character CKAN package ID / slug (e.g. "taxation-statistics-2022-23", "corporate-transparency").

title: Fetch CKAN metadata for an ATO dataset — ato_meta
text: list elements: id: CKAN package slug title: human-readable title notes: dataset description (truncated 400 chars) licence: licence title metadata_modified: ISO timestamp last CKAN update n_resources: number downloadable files resource_urls: character vector resource URLs

what: Reconcile an aggregate against Commonwealth budget totals — ato_reconcile
title: Reconcile an aggregate against Commonwealth budget totals — ato_reconcile
text: Compares scalar (data frame total) published Final Budget Outcome figure year revenue line. Useful sanity check ATO Taxation Statistics sum reporting paper brief.

title: Reconcile an aggregate against Commonwealth budget totals — ato_reconcile
code: ato_reconcile(value, year, measure, sum_column = NULL)

title: Reconcile an aggregate against Commonwealth budget totals — ato_reconcile
text: value Numeric; figure check, AUD (AUD billions). ato_tbl can also passed: pass sum_column pick numeric column sum. year Financial year, e.g. "2022-23". measure One measure codes ato_crosswalk("budget"), example "individuals_income_tax_net", "company_tax_net", "gst_net", "fuel_excise_net". sum_column Column name sum value data frame. Default NULL (errors multiple numeric columns exist).

title: Reconcile an aggregate against Commonwealth budget totals — ato_reconcile
text: one-row data frame: measure, year, value_aud, reference_aud, diff_aud, pct_diff, source. Emits warning abs(pct_diff) > 0.05.

title: Reconcile an aggregate against Commonwealth budget totals — ato_reconcile
text: Discrepancies ATO Taxation Statistics aggregates Final Budget Outcome (FBO) expected meaningful: Taxation Statistics based assessments made cut-date (usually October following calendar year) may exclude late-lodging returns. FBO figures cash-basis Commonwealth receipts; Taxation Statistics accrual-basis tax assessed. GST, excise, fuel credits timing refund effects distort cash-vs-assessment gap. 1-3 per cent gap consistent accrual--cash reconciliation Treasury publishes FBO statement revenues; larger gaps warrant investigation. bundled reference totals inst/extdata/budget_reference_totals.csv taken relevant FBO release, precise table cited source column row.

title: Reconcile an aggregate against Commonwealth budget totals — ato_reconcile
text: Commonwealth Australia (various years). Final Budget Outcome. Treasury, Canberra. https://budget.gov.au/content/fbo/index.htm Australian Bureau Statistics (various years). Taxation Revenue, Australia. Catalogue 5506.0. Australian Taxation Office (annual). Australian tax gaps – overview, methodology notes accrual-vs-cash reconciliation.

title: Reconcile an aggregate against Commonwealth budget totals — ato_reconcile
code: ato_reconcile(value = 316.4e9,               year = "2022-23",               measure = "individuals_income_tax_net") #>                      measure    year value_aud reference_aud diff_aud pct_diff #> 1 individuals_income_tax_net 2022-23 3.164e+11     3.164e+11        0        0 #>                                   source #> 1 Final Budget Outcome 2022-23 Table 4.2

title: Corporate Tax Transparency — ato_top_taxpayers
text: Returns ATO's annual Corporate Tax Transparency release, mandated Part 5-25 Taxation Administration Act 1953. Covers every Australian public company, foreign-owned company, Australian-owned private company AUD 100 million total-income threshold (private-company threshold lowered AUD 200 million AUD 100 million 2022-23 income year onwards, making three categories uniform). 2023-24 release published 1 October 2025 covered 4,110 entities.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-28 Initial CRAN submission. First public release. Provides R access Australian Taxation Office public datasets via data.gov.au Comprehensive Knowledge Archive Network (CKAN) API, reproducibility, harmonisation, interop tooling.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_catalog(): list ATO datasets data.gov.au resources, licences, modification dates. ato_download(): generic resource downloader local cache.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_individuals(): Taxation Statistics Individual snapshot (Table 1). ato_individuals_postcode(): individual tax return items postcode state (Individuals Table 6). Accepts vector years (e.g. year = 2018:2022) returns stacked panel year column. ato_individuals_occupation(): individual tax return items occupation (ANZSCO), sex, income range. ato_individuals_age(), ato_individuals_sex(), ato_individuals_state(): demographic cuts.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_companies(): Company Taxation Statistics across nine Company Tables via table = ...: snapshot, key_items_by_size, entity_type, industry (default), industry_by_size, sub_industry, taxable_status, source, expenses. ato_super_funds(): APRA-regulated superannuation fund Self-Managed Superannuation Fund (SMSF) aggregates.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_top_taxpayers(): Corporate Tax Transparency release sheet switch income_tax prrt (Petroleum Resource Rent Tax filers). ato_gst(): Goods Services Tax tables Activity Statement Ratios. ato_industry(): industry-level aggregates derived Individual Company tables.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_tax_gaps(): annual Tax Gap estimates across tax heads (individuals, small business, large corporate, GST, excise, PRRT, superannuation guarantee). Treasury cites series every MYEFO. ato_rdti(): Research Development Tax Incentive claimants, expenditure, offset data. ato_irpd(): International Related Party Dealings across annual packages 2019-20 (Table 1 totals, Table 2 jurisdiction, Table 3 chart-data index). Core BEPS transfer-pricing dataset.

what: Excise, small business, and education loans
title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_excise(): excise rate schedule, Fuel Tax Credit rates, beer clearances, spirits excisable beverages. ato_sme_benchmarks(): Small Business Benchmarks (industry-specific cost--turnover related ratios). ato_help(): Study Training Support Loan statistics covering HELP, AASL (Australian Apprenticeship Support Loans), VSL (VET Student Loans).

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_tax_expenditures(): Treasury Tax Expenditures Insights Statement (TEIS). ato_fuel_tax_credits(): FTC claims rates. ato_division293(): Division 293 additional contributions tax data. ato_prrt(): Petroleum Resource Rent Tax aggregates. ato_compliance(): ATO compliance activity statistics. ato_medicare_levy(): Medicare Levy Surcharge aggregates. ato_whm(): Working Holiday Maker tax filings.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_state_tax(): ABS 5506.0 state territory taxation. ato_international(): OECD Revenue Statistics. ato_rba(): RBA H1 Commonwealth Government Receipts.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_snapshot(): pin session snapshot date, recorded every ato_tbl provenance header, manifest entry, citation. ato_sha256(): SHA-256 digest file -memory object. Cached downloads carry sidecar hash verified every cache hit; drift warns hash prefixes. ato_manifest(), ato_manifest_clear(), ato_manifest_write(): session registry every fetch (URL, CKAN IDs, SHA-256, size, timestamp, snapshot pin, R ato versions). Output data frame, YAML, JSON, CSV paper appendices. ato_deposit_zenodo(): stage Zenodo deposit payload session manifest. Dry run default; call upload = TRUE ZENODO_TOKEN mint DOI. ato_cite(): citation helper producing plain text, BibTeX, APA output ato_tbl URL, optional SHA-256 digest, snapshot date, doi = argument.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_crosswalk(): bundled classification reference tables (ANZSIC 2006 2020, ANZSCO 2013 2021, postcode-state anchors, ABS CPI annual, ABS ERP annual, Final Budget Outcome reference totals). ato_harmonise(): rename columns canonical names across multi-year panels using ATO_COL_VARIANTS. ato_reconcile(): compare aggregate published Final Budget Outcome figure year measure; warns gaps 5 per cent. ato_deflate(): nominal AUD real AUD base year using bundled ABS CPI. ato_per_capita(): divide ABS ERP.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_to_taxstats(): rename columns ATO aggregate schema taxstats 2 per cent microdata sample schema, either direction. ato_schema_map(): return full column-name mapping.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ato_cache_info(), ato_clear_cache(): cache management.

title: ato 0.1.0
text: ATO confidentiality-suppression tokens (np, n.p., *, others) coerced NA ato_fetch_csv() ato_fetch_xlsx() numeric columns stay numeric. XLSX header auto-detection: scans 15 rows find column-header row, handling ATO workbooks lead title, narrative, Notes sheet. Every returned ato_tbl carries provenance attributes (source URL, CC licence, retrieval time, title) exposed via print() ato_cite().

title: ato 0.1.0
text: Reproducibility workflow (snapshot, manifest, SHA-256, Zenodo deposit). Panels, harmonisation, reconciliation, real terms, per capita. Interop grattan taxstats: costing hypothetical reform. Canonical replications: top 1 per cent income share, corporate ETR industry, tax gap trend, HELP debt age cohort.
